data.title: NHPC Shares Decline by Over 3% During Today's Session
data.description:
Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

During today's session, NHPC shares experienced a decline of 3.28%, with the price falling to Rs 77.65. This movement marks a notable shift in the stock's performance.

The decline in NHPC's stock price occurs within the context of its presence in the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index. The stock's performance is influenced by broader market dynamics and sector-specific trends.

Financial Performance


NHPC's financial performance provides insights into its recent operational and economic activities. Key financial metrics include revenue, net profit, and earnings per share (EPS).

Quarterly Performance:


  • December 2023: Revenue was Rs 2,055.50 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 627.76 Cr, and EPS was Rs 0.49.
  • March 2024: Revenue decreased to Rs 1,888.14 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 607.21 Cr, and EPS was Rs 0.55.
  • June 2024: Revenue increased to Rs 2,694.20 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 1,107.75 Cr, and EPS was Rs 1.02.
  • September 2024: Revenue further increased to Rs 3,051.93 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 1,068.93 Cr, and EPS was Rs 0.90.
  • December 2024: Revenue decreased to Rs 2,286.76 Cr, Net Profit significantly dropped to Rs 328.94 Cr, and EPS was Rs 0.23.

Annual Performance:


  • 2020: Revenue was Rs 10,007.81 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 3,341.87 Cr, and EPS was Rs 2.87.
  • 2021: Revenue was Rs 9,647.89 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 3,587.62 Cr, and EPS was Rs 3.24.
  • 2022: Revenue was Rs 9,188.78 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 3,775.72 Cr, and EPS was Rs 4.71.
  • 2023: Revenue increased to Rs 10,607.40 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 4,239.83 Cr, and EPS was Rs 3.87.
  • 2024: Revenue decreased to Rs 9,632.16 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 4,023.61 Cr, and EPS was Rs 3.61.

Financial Ratios:


Key financial ratios provide additional context to NHPC's performance:
  • Basic EPS (Rs.): Decreased from Rs 3.87 in March 2023 to Rs 3.61 in March 2024.
  • Book Value [Excl. Reval Reserve]/Share (Rs.): Increased from Rs 41.53 in March 2023 to Rs 43.70 in March 2024.
  • Dividend/Share (Rs.): Increased from Rs 1.85 in March 2023 to Rs 1.90 in March 2024.
  • Debt to Equity (x): Increased from 0.80 in March 2023 to 0.84 in March 2024.

Market Sentiment


Recent sentiment analysis indicates a bearish outlook on NHPC as of May 6, 2025, according to Moneycontrol analysis. This sentiment may be influencing the stock's current price movement. With the stock's current price at Rs 77.65, NHPC's performance reflects a mix of financial results and market sentiment during today's session.

data.title: Defence stocks gain amidst escalating India-Pakistan tension, HAL, BEL up 2%
data.description:
In a recent report, analysts at Nuvama had noted that while the nation’s defence spending of around 2.3 percent of GDP is lower than global defence majors ( around 3–5% of GDP), they expect defence capital outlay to grow 7–8% annually over the next five years

Defence stocks opened in the green on Friday after another day of escalating tensions along the India -Pakistan Border. On Thursday night, according to government officials, India neutralised Pakistan military's attempt to hit military stations in Jammu, Pathankot, Udhampur and some other locations with missiles and drones. At open the  broader Nifty Defence index also gained nearly 1.28 percent.

Shares of Paras Defence jumped 3.52 percent, Hindustan Aeronautics Ltd (HAL) rose 1.6 percent and Bharat Electronics Ltd (BEL) gained 2.28 percent, Zen Technologies gained around 4.99 percent, Mazgaon Dock also gained around 0.85 percent.

Follow the Moneycontrol live blog for all market updates

On May 8, shares of defence companies tumbled in the second half amidst profit booking with most defence players losing early gains. At close, the Index was down by nearly one percent.

Also read: Samir Arora says war risk a 1% tail event, can’t position for that; India theme strong for foreign investors

In a recent report, analysts at Nuvama had noted that while the nation’s defence spending of around 2.3 percent of GDP is lower than global defence majors ( around 3–5% of GDP), they expect defence capital outlay to grow 7–8% annually over the next five years, "potentially translating to more than $130 billion of procurement given dynamic geopolitical and regional scenarios."

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: NIFTY MIDCAP 150 Index Live, 09 May 2025: NHPC sees price fall by 3%
data.description:
During today's session, the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 is currently trading at 19,333.95, down by 257.85 points, a fall of 1.32 percent. The NIFTY MIDCAP 150 Index opened at 19,165.40, hitting a high of 19,479.85 and a low of 19,165.10. The 52-week high for the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 Index is 22,515.40, and the 52-week low is 17,269.50.

The NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index has exhibited a YTD return of -8.56 percent, a 1-week return of -2.06 percent, and a 1-month return of 5.79 percent. The 3-month return is -2.47 percent, and the 6-month return is -7.60 percent. Over the past year, the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index has shown a return of 5.95 percent. The two and three-year returns for the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 Index are 58.65 percent and 83.30 percent, respectively.

Technical indicators for the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 Index show the following: The SMA50 is 18,957.07, and the SMA200 is 20,505.43. The RSI (14) is at 48.14. The MACD (12,26,9) is 195.23. The Stochastic (20,3) is 58.23, the ROC (20) is 7.47, the CCI (20) is -31.40, and the ADX (14) is 12.45.
